features.title: A platform built for objective progress

pvp.title: Strict mock with watermark
pvp.desc: Solve twice: strict (no AI, Cue blocked server-side) and AI-mode (with hints). Watermark separates 'honest' from 'with AI' — that's an objective readiness currency.

ai.title: AI-coach with memory
ai.desc: 4-layer memory: snapshot · facts · summary · episodes. The coach remembers your context between sessions — not yet another GPT chat.

daily.title: Hone — daily ritual
daily.desc: Quiet desktop cockpit: AI plan, focus, notes with AI-link. Works offline, syncs via outbox.

cohorts.title: Tutor toolkit (free)
cohorts.desc: Student snapshots, AI pre-session brief, shared calendar, library, session notes. No money step in between.

atlas.desc: Track progress map. Ranking-proxy curation: link the best external resources (Strang, mlcourse, DDIA) — don't clone them.

tracks.subtitle: Multi-track Atlas: keep «Senior dev + English» as a sticky combo. Each track has its own mock rubric, Insights, Atlas subgraph. The primary track defines defaults; the others live alongside.
tracks.footer_motto: PICK AT SIGN-UP · CHANGE IN SETTINGS · INSIGHTS EVERYWHERE
tracks.dev_sub: Algorithms · SQL · Go · System Design · Behavioral. A starting point for mid-level engineers. With an ML Platform branch (K8s deep, Airflow, model serving, at-least-once).
tracks.dev_senior_sub: Staff/principal-level System Design + Tech Lead / EM behavioral.
tracks.ml_sub: Senior ML/MLE — classical ML, DL, transformers, LLM/GenAI, ML system design, MLOps. 9-step curated path + 5-axis radar + ml-coach with memory.
tracks.english_sub: Not Duolingo. A discipline layer between you and your tutor.
tracks.sysanalyst_sub: Requirements · UML/BPMN · integration · SQL · process. For switchers from dev.
tracks.product_analyst_sub: Metrics · A/B + CUPED · SQL · RICE/JTBD · insight comms. Cheaper than GoPractice.
tracks.qa_sub: Test design · API testing · automation · bug RCA · process. No content-expert needed.
tracks.devops_sub: Infra · observability · CI/CD · incident · security. Whiteboard-precision interviews.

insights.subtitle: Focus hours from Hone, mock results from druz9.online, stuck patterns from Cue — in one analytics layer. The «honest vs with AI» watermark makes progress objective, not self-rated.
insights.footer_motto: AGGREGATES THREE CLIENTS · UPDATES HOURLY · OPENS IN BROWSER
insights.card1_note: Focus hours from Hone + mock sessions on druz9.online. No guesses.
insights.card2_note: Strict mock without AI. Cue is disabled server-side — you can't bypass it by modifying the client.
insights.card3_note: Skill Atlas updates from solves and mock rubric. You see where to dig.

pricing.footnote: Cancel anytime · Roubles · Team invoicing · Data stored in Russia (152-FZ)

faq.title: Real questions.
faq.q1_a: Every mode of work has its own mental space. Web is loud: mock, atlas, analytics, Codex. The cockpit is quiet: your plan, your notes, focus. The whisper is invisible: help without breaking flow. Merge them and they all blur.
faq.q2_a: No. Start with druz9.online — run your first strict mock. Add Hone when the daily ritual matters. Add Cue when you first get stuck in a real interview.
faq.q3_q: What's in free and where does Pro start?
faq.q3_a: Free forever: 1 mock per week, basic Atlas, Codex, tutor toolkit for educators. Pro (990 ₽/mo) includes unlimited mock, AI-tutor with memory, AI planning in Hone, unlimited Cue and Insights. Cue requires Pro on launch — it's the main paying hook.
faq.q4_q: How does AI-mock differ from strict-mock?
faq.q4_a: AI mode — chat helper on the right, a «hint» button, like training. Strict mode — just you, the task, the timer; Cue is blocked server-side during it. The watermark on the result splits «honest» from «with AI» — that turns the score into an objective readiness metric.
faq.q5_a: No card required. Unlocks all Pro features: unlimited mock, AI planner, Cue. After it ends — auto-rollback to Free. No dark patterns with «forgot to cancel — charged».
faq.q6_a: Right now macOS (arm64 + x64), notarized DMG. Windows port — Q3 2026. Linux community port tracked on GitHub. The druz9.online site runs in the browser without install.
faq.q7_a: Depends on your contract and the meeting. The stealth feature exists for legitimate scenarios: open-plan offices, helping yourself on a hard step, recording your own meetings for review. On strict mock sessions Cue is blocked server-side — that's part of the ecosystem's honesty. The ToS spells out user responsibility.
faq.q8_a: Primary storage in Moscow, 152-FZ. EU replica for speed if the team is abroad. Notes and Whiteboard in Hone live locally by default — they only reach the server if you press sync yourself.
faq.q9_a: Anytime from Settings. Access remains until the end of the paid period. Refunds within the first 14 days — write to t.me/druz9.

products.atlas_desc: Solve each task twice: strict (no AI, Cue disabled server-side) and AI-mode (with hints). The watermark stitches the delta into the result — that's an objective readiness currency, not self-rating. Multi-track Atlas, AI tutor 24/7, Codex as the team's voice.
products.hone_desc: A minimalist desktop app for quiet work. AI plans the day, tracks streaks, stays out of the way. Pure black. Keyboard. No noise.
products.cue_desc: A quiet AI companion for interview prep, open-plan offices and live meeting transcripts. ⌘⇧Space — screenshot, question, persona. Visibility toggle in Settings: show or hide the window during screen-share.

hone.insight_suffix: and English conditionals up. SD still sagging — I'll suggest one sysdesign task for tomorrow.
